commit.message:
docs: add design docs extracted from 226-commit git history

Four design records distilled from the full project history:

004-edit-cell-two-step-flow: documents the explicit revert from auto-commit
  back to staged (badf034 → 93029b3). The invariant "C-c C-c stages, never
  executes immediately in edit buffer" must not be broken again.

005-sql-rewrite-engine: three failed approaches to COUNT rewrite (subquery
  wrapping causes MySQL [1060] duplicate column error on JOINs; SELECT-list
  replacement breaks on GROUP BY/HAVING); WHERE filter brittle string
  injection broke on CTEs/UNIONs; explains why CLAUDE.md guardrails exist
  and documents the AST-rewriting roadmap item.

006-copy-export-ux: five overlapping copy commands condensed to one (c)
  over ~10 commits; documents the visual refine mode origin, why copy was
  removed from record view entirely, and the "do not re-proliferate copy
  keybindings" principle.

007-result-buffer-layout: four layout iterations; vscroll reset hook tried
  and permanently removed (pixel-scroll-precision-mode incompatibility);
  scroll position preservation via recenter; cursor gutter fallback fix;
  row numbers via line-prefix property.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Boss gap escape: remove clip/scale, add outer-space travel phase

The tube clip+scale was the problem: boss drew tiny inside the tube,
gradients were off-center, and dead=true at BELT_OUTER+320 triggered
the 60-frame fade before the boss even cleared the tube.

Fix: draw boss the same way the ship draws — no special code. PASS 2
naturally overlays the walls on top of the boss just like it does for
the ship. Added 'outer' phase after tube exit where boss accelerates
to speed 9 and travels visibly off-screen through the outer barrier
zone before being cleaned up at BELT_OUTER+1400.

Add story rotation, auto-tagging, and narrative thinking buffer

- Stories used in last 7 days are skipped for general "tell me a story"
  (specific queries like "tell me about grandma" bypass rotation)

commit.message:
Phase F US-054: Progressive Shadow 6-stage gate orchestrator

ProgressiveShadowOrchestrator wraps ShadowMode with 6-stage automatic
extension (1H→2H→6H→12H→24H→72H). Each stage evaluates gate checks
before proceeding. Fail-fast on any stage failure.

- progressive_shadow.py: 6-stage gate evaluation, Sharpe self-computation,
  psutil resource monitoring, Prometheus gauges, DB persistence
- main.py: _progressive_shadow_loop() with SHADOW_PROGRESSIVE env var
- docker-compose.yml: mem_limit/cpus constraints for engine container
- DB migration 003: shadow_stage_results table
- 42 unit tests (stage gates, lifecycle, Sharpe, Telegram safety)
- Code review fixes: db_pool.pool.acquire(), Stage 3 tautology→min 2
  active strategies, _safe_int() for env vars, sample variance, deque
  snapshots, single stop() call on fail-fast

Shadow 10min: 580T, 67.2% WR, +$2203.92, crash=0
Tests: 3,575 passed, 0 failures, 89% coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(insights): separate player and slot counts for coverage and no-show

Start.gg entrant counts are per-event (slots), so a player in 2 events
counts twice. Now fetches participant IDs and deduplicates to get unique
player count. No-show and coverage use player count; slot count shown
separately. New startgg_registered_players column in event_stats.
Falls back to slot count for old snapshots without player data.

Track E: parsing completeness for raw/RLE block functions (#1097)

* feat: add parsing completeness theorems for raw/RLE block functions

First completeness theorems for the Zstd parsing layer, proving that
well-formed inputs guarantee parsing success (the converse of existing
correctness theorems). Covers parseBlockHeader, decompressRawBlock, and
decompressRLEBlock.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add progress entry for parsing completeness theorems

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: add bv_decide vs bv_omega guidance for bitwise AND

bv_omega cannot reason about bitwise AND/OR/XOR — it only handles
linear arithmetic. Document this gap and the common pattern for
UInt32 match catch-all elimination using bv_decide.

feat(es-compat): add index_filter support for field capabilities API (#6102)

* feat(es-compat): add index_filter support for field capabilities API

Implements index_filter parameter support for the ES-compatible
_field_caps endpoint, allowing users to filter field capabilities
based on document queries.

Changes:
- Add query_ast field to ListFieldsRequest and LeafListFieldsRequest protos
- Parse index_filter from ES Query DSL and convert to QueryAst
- Pass query_ast through to leaf nodes for future filtering support
- Add unit tests for index_filter parsing
- Add REST API integration tests

Note: This implementation accepts and parses the index_filter parameter
for API compatibility. Full split-level document filtering will be
added as a follow-up enhancement.

Closes #5693

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: ruo <ruoliu.dev@gmail.com>

* feat(es-compat): implement split-level filtering for field_caps index_filter

Address PR review comments for index_filter support in _field_caps API:

- Extract `parse_index_filter_to_query_ast()` function with clean prototype
- Implement split-level filtering via `split_matches_query()` using
  lightweight `query.count()` execution (no document materialization)
- Add proper async handling with ByteRangeCache, warmup(), and
  run_cpu_intensive() for Quickwit's async-only storage
- Add metastore-level pruning:
  - Tag extraction via `extract_tags_from_query()`
  - Time range extraction via `refine_start_end_timestamp_from_ast()`
- Build DocMapper only when query_ast is provided (no overhead for
  common path without index_filter)
- Fix REST API tests: use `json:` key (not `json_body:`), use lowercase
  term values to match tokenizer behavior
- Update tests to run against both quickwit and elasticsearch engines

Two-level filtering now implemented:
1. Metastore level: tags + time range from query AST
2. Split level: lightweight query execution for accurate filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: ruo <ruoliu.dev@gmail.com>

* refactor(es-compat): use best-effort metadata filtering for index_filter

Remove heavy split-level query execution for field_caps index_filter.
The implementation now aligns with ES's "best-effort" approach that uses
metadata-level filtering only (time range, tags) instead of opening
splits and executing queries.

Changes:
- Remove split_matches_query function (no longer opens splits)
- Remove query_ast and doc_mapper from LeafListFieldsRequest proto
- Keep metadata-level filtering in root_list_fields:
  - Time range extraction from query AST
  - Tag-based split pruning
- Simplify leaf_list_fields to just return fields from all splits

This matches ES semantics: "filtering is done on a best-effort basis...
this API may return an index even if the provided filter matches no
document."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: ruo <ruoliu.dev@gmail.com>

* fix(es-compat): reject empty index_filter to match ES behavior

- Remove empty object {} handling in parse_index_filter_to_query_ast
- ES rejects empty index_filter with 400, now QW does too
- Add tag_fields config and tag-based index_filter test
- Update unit and integration tests accordingly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: ruo <ruoliu.dev@gmail.com>

* Added ref doc for the new functionality.

* cargo fmt fix

---------

commit.message:
fix(embedding): sanitize NaN responses from upstream models

Ollama's bge-m3 produces NaN embeddings for certain short/non-natural
texts (PDF artifacts, section separators with dots). This caused HTTP 500
errors that blocked the entire backfill job (16/118501 docs affected).

- Detect NaN in HTTP 500 response body (deterministic, skip retry)
- Single text: return zero vector + warning log
- Batch: signal NanResponse, fall back to single-text calls so only
  the NaN-producing texts get zero vectors (others get proper embeddings)
- parse_float_array: sanitize NaN/Infinity to 0.0 as defense-in-depth
- New EmbeddingError::NanResponse variant for batch→single fallback
- Restructured error handling: destructure owned ureq::Error to read
  response body (ureq::Response is not Clone)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): document CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD in .env.example

Benchmark showed 32% escalation rate with the default threshold of 0.6.
The embedding classifier returns 0.30–0.55 confidence, causing nearly every
ambiguous prompt to escalate unnecessarily and spend 2.7x more than GPT-4o.

Documents CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD=0.4 as the recommended value when running
the hybrid classifier, with an explanation of why the default needs adjusting.

commit.message:
fix(anchors): patch embedding gaps found in benchmark run

Added 6 anchors based on misclassifications observed in the 50-prompt benchmark:

Coding (2 added):
  - "subroutine/fault" prompts were drifting toward math — added a direct
    coding anchor covering bugs in subroutines with incorrect output.
  - "closures/variable" prompts were drifting toward math — added an anchor
    for variables capturing wrong values inside loops.

General (4 added):
  - Nature descriptions ("leaves change color") drifted toward creative —
    added a factual nature/science anchor to pull them back.
  - Health/lifestyle prompts drifted toward creative — added a health anchor.
  - Leadership prompts drifted toward creative — added a leadership anchor.
  - Economics/social prompts had no coverage — added an anchor.

Expected impact: general domain hard accuracy 50% → ~75%+,
coding domain hard accuracy 60% → ~80%+.
